pattern: Since consonants never move, pulling out just the vowels, sorting that smaller collection by character code, and dropping the sorted values back into the same vowel slots rebuilds the answer directly.
hints: Consonants never move at all, so you only ever need to think about the positions that hold vowels.
Collect the characters at every vowel position into their own list and sort that list by character code -- both lowercase and uppercase vowels are compared this way.
Walk through the original string again, and each time you reach a vowel position, take the next value off the sorted list instead of the original character.

constraints.note: letters of the English alphabet, upper and lower case

edge_inputs.description: statement example, mixed case vowels reordered
statement example, no vowels at all
every character is a vowel in mixed case, testing full ASCII ordering
the same vowel repeated in both cases, uppercase must sort before lowercase
ordinary mixed sentence-like string with consonants and vowels
vowels already in sorted order should come back unchanged

stress.description: random mixed-case letters across the full allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    return [''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))]
